name: Apple Watch
short_description: A wrist-worn smartwatch that fuses health and motion sensing, on-device intelligence, and a voice assistant into an always-on body-worn device.
long_description: Announced in 2014 and released in 2015, the Apple Watch is a wrist-worn computer with optical heart-rate, accelerometer, gyroscope, and (in later models) ECG, blood-oxygen, and temperature sensors, plus the Siri voice assistant and increasingly on-device machine learning. It matters for physical AI because it is one of the most widely worn sensing-and-inference platforms in daily life: it continuously reads the body, runs models for fall detection, irregular-rhythm and crash detection, and activity coaching, and nudges the wearer through ambient haptics and notifications. As such it exemplifies how AI becomes legible not as a robot but as a quiet, always-present body-worn agent.

images.caption: An Apple Watch Series 7 with a digital watch face.
images.alt_text: An Apple Watch with a rounded-square screen showing a clock face, worn on a wrist.

sources.title: Apple Watch
sources.access_notes: Confirms announcement September 2014 and release April 24, 2015, plus sensor and feature history.
